jokes.text: Thanks to the magic of Disney Animatronics, Strom Thurmond will live another 100 years.
jokes.setup: Announcement at Republican Party meeting about new business
jokes.punchline: Using Disney technology to keep an elderly politician alive for 100 more years
jokes.explanation: Dark political satire combining Disney with keeping ancient politicians alive - original concept with good economy

jokes.text: Twenty more terms!
jokes.setup: Following the Thurmond animatronics announcement
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic response wanting even more terms
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation of the previous joke but predictable

jokes.text: What act of unmitigated evil shall the Republican Party undertake this week?
jokes.setup: Moving to new business at party meeting
jokes.punchline: Casually referring to their agenda as 'unmitigated evil'
jokes.explanation: Self-aware evil with good economy and quotable phrasing

jokes.text: You've already done enough, Nader.
jokes.setup: Someone trying to contribute to evil Republican agenda
jokes.punchline: Dismissing Ralph Nader as having already helped Republicans enough
jokes.explanation: Sharp political observation about Nader's spoiler effect, excellent economy

jokes.text: Those lousy Muppets have been taking food out of my mouth for too long!
jokes.setup: Suggestion to get rid of PBS
jokes.punchline: Personal vendetta against Muppets for economic reasons
jokes.explanation: Absurd personal motivation for political position - solid character comedy

jokes.text: Back in Texas, we got rid of it, and it made everyone a lot happier. He was so happy...
jokes.setup: Discussing getting rid of the environment
jokes.punchline: Claiming eliminating environment made people happy, with ominous trailing off
jokes.explanation: Dark environmental humor with unsettling implication in the trailing off

jokes.text: Now Bob Dole will read from the Necronomicon.
jokes.setup: Moving to next agenda item
jokes.punchline: Having Bob Dole read from fictional evil book of the dead
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation mixing mundane political figure with supernatural horror

jokes.text: Recycling a felony. And Smokey the Bear is now Choppy the Lumberjack.
jokes.setup: Reading environmental policy changes
jokes.punchline: Complete reversal of environmental mascot from protector to destroyer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of beloved environmental icon - highly quotable and memorable

jokes.text: A grizzly bear with a chainsaw. Now there's a killing machine!
jokes.setup: Responding to Choppy the Lumberjack
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic approval of the absurdly dangerous combination
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the absurdist concept with nice economy

jokes.text: Stop endangering yourself. Stop endangering yourself.
jokes.setup: Warning message playing
jokes.punchline: Safety warning in increasingly dangerous environment
jokes.explanation: Basic ironic contrast but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Good Lord! Acid rain! I'm singing in the rain Just singing in the rain What a glorious feeling
jokes.setup: Acid rain falling
jokes.punchline: Homer cheerfully singing 'Singing in the Rain' despite it being acid
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer characterization - oblivious optimism in face of danger, good structure

jokes.text: It burns like a Glasgow bikini wax!
jokes.setup: Reacting to acid rain
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific Scottish comparison involving painful grooming
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Willie-esque line with excellent character integration and memorable specificity

jokes.text: We'd better stay inside, at least until the squirrels stop melting.
jokes.setup: Suggesting they go inside due to acid rain
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of melting squirrels as a weather condition to wait out
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark absurdist humor delivered with Marge's characteristic deadpan practicality

jokes.text: He smelled like puke!
jokes.setup: Male date says he felt a connection and wants to see her again
jokes.punchline: Brutal honest assessment completely contradicting his enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection with good structure and economy, though concept is familiar

jokes.text: The game is crazy enough as it is. How can an iron be a landlord?
jokes.setup: Choosing to play original Monopoly
jokes.punchline: Questioning the basic absurdity of Monopoly game pieces
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about familiar game absurdity, fits Marge's practical nature

jokes.text: They told me it would be like this on the outside.
jokes.setup: Lisa not trusting him due to jail time
jokes.punchline: Homer treating family Monopoly game like post-prison life
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the jail theme with typical Homer self-pity

jokes.text: These hotels are made of Legos. Bart, you're cheating!
jokes.setup: Playing Monopoly
jokes.punchline: Discovering Bart has substituted Lego buildings for real game pieces
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for both Bart's mischief and Lisa's rule-following nature

jokes.text: Oh, sure. You take his side just because he bought you that house on St. James Place.
jokes.setup: Marge defending Bart's Lego hotels as probably accidental
jokes.punchline: Lisa accusing Marge of corruption over a Monopoly property
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how seriously Lisa takes games and rules

jokes.text: Mom, that's not how you pry them apart. I've been prying them apart since before you were born!
jokes.setup: Marge trying to separate fighting Homer and Bart
jokes.punchline: Lisa correcting her mother's technique and claiming more experience
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Lisa's maturity and the family's dysfunction as normal

jokes.text: The Prison Nutcracker Suite is one week away. And I don't see five Sugar Plums. I see five guys who don't know their moves and don't seem to care!
jokes.setup: Police practicing for performance
jokes.punchline: Wiggum treating tough cops like prima ballerinas in Nutcracker
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist concept perfectly fitting Wiggum's character - cops doing ballet

jokes.text: And don't try anything, because Johanssen there is a snitch.
jokes.setup: Leaving for domestic disturbance call
jokes.punchline: Casual betrayal of officer's trust in front of everyone
jokes.explanation: Good Wiggum moment showing his poor leadership skills with nice economy

jokes.text: Another case of Monopoly-related violence, Chief.
jokes.setup: Arriving at Simpson house disturbance
jokes.punchline: Treating board game violence as a recognized crime category
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan delivery suggesting this is a common occurrence, great economy

jokes.text: How do those Parker Brothers sleep at night?
jokes.setup: Learning about Monopoly violence
jokes.punchline: Blaming board game manufacturers for family fights
jokes.explanation: Good callback escalation with Wiggum's typical misdirected blame

jokes.text: I am Brenda. I am programmed to talk in a calm and constructive manner. Destroy! Destroy!
jokes.setup: Robot introduced to defuse domestic situations peacefully
jokes.punchline: Immediately malfunctioning to become violent
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with classic robot malfunction trope but well-executed

jokes.text: Hey, this is taffy! Police brutality!
jokes.setup: Robot apparently attacking Homer
jokes.punchline: Complaining it's just taffy but still calling it brutality
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection revealing harmless outcome but Homer still complaining

jokes.text: And chew-tality!
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about taffy police brutality
jokes.punchline: Extending the complaint with a pun on 'brutality'
jokes.explanation: Decent pun but relies on wordplay crutch, predictable follow-up

jokes.text: Too bad real women don't come with these, huh?
jokes.setup: Commenting on robot's off switch
jokes.punchline: Wishing real women had off switches
jokes.explanation: Dated sexist humor that relies on cheap misogyny rather than wit

jokes.text: You're going to Stanford. You're going to Stanford.
jokes.setup: Lisa worried about getting into Ivy League after arrest
jokes.punchline: Marge repeatedly insisting on Stanford as if it's punishment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's limited knowledge of prestigious schools

jokes.text: Stanford. This family has hit rock bottom.
jokes.setup: Marge insisting Lisa will go to Stanford
jokes.punchline: Lisa treating Stanford like a terrible fate
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Lisa's academic snobbery and family dysfunction

jokes.text: Like Denzel Washington in The Preacher's Wife, or Will Smith in Bagger Vance, or Slimer in Ghostbusters.
jokes.setup: Homer insisting Gabriel is an angel like movie characters
jokes.punchline: Including non-angel Slimer in list of angel characters
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's movie knowledge and faulty logic

jokes.text: Don't blame me. Blame Tinseltown in its second golden age, may it never end.
jokes.setup: Marge blaming Homer for seeing too many movies
jokes.punchline: Homer's grandiose defense of contemporary Hollywood
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's earnest appreciation for movies

jokes.text: I got sprayed by a skunk! Let me rub it off on your sweaters! Just 10 more years. Just 10 more years.
jokes.setup: Marge describing her room as sanctuary that family respects
jokes.punchline: Bart immediately violating it, Marge counting down to empty nest
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Marge's patience wearing thin

jokes.text: He has to be here. I'm just that nuts.
jokes.setup: Introducing Gabriel as his social worker
jokes.punchline: Homer matter-of-factly acknowledging his mental state
jokes.explanation: Good Homer self-awareness moment with excellent economy

jokes.text: How come you get a social worker? I'm the one with stigmata.
jokes.setup: Learning Homer has a social worker
jokes.punchline: Bart claiming to have stigmata as if it's a competition
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist line perfectly capturing Bart's attention-seeking and sibling rivalry

jokes.text: Because at 6:00, we go live!
jokes.setup: Marge explaining why she makes practice dinners
jokes.punchline: Treating family dinner like a live television broadcast
jokes.explanation: Brilliant characterization of Marge's domestic perfectionism using TV metaphor

jokes.text: It's where I go to drink alcohol, which is the mortal equivalent of your ambrosia.
jokes.setup: Showing Gabriel a bar
jokes.punchline: Homer's pretentious explanation of drinking as divine experience
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer trying to sound sophisticated about his drinking

jokes.text: Well, not with that temper.
jokes.setup: Gabriel angrily insisting he's not an angel
jokes.punchline: Homer suggesting angels should be more pleasant
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response showing Homer's unshakeable belief and logic

jokes.text: Marge, Bart, Girl Bart, the one who doesn't talk, and the fat guy. How I loathe him!
jokes.setup: Describing his family to Gabriel
jokes.punchline: Referring to himself as 'the fat guy' and expressing self-loathing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-referential humor with Homer's lack of self-awareness followed by sudden insight

jokes.text: Well, this is one family that doesn't swing that way!
jokes.setup: Gabriel suggesting they need to cooperate
jokes.punchline: Homer misunderstanding 'swing' in sexual context
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer misunderstanding but relies on sexual innuendo for the laugh

jokes.text: As a family, we drive away. We cover for each other as a family. That's what Gabriel would've wanted
jokes.setup: Family facing danger, need a plan
jokes.punchline: Homer's plan is cowardly abandonment while invoking Gabriel's memory
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer cowardice disguised as family solidarity and respect for the dead.

jokes.text: Look, Gabriel! We're learning to work together! That's great. But so are the wolves and cougars!
jokes.setup: Lisa proudly announcing family cooperation
jokes.punchline: Gabriel pointing out predators are also cooperating to eat them
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection deflating the proud moment with immediate danger

jokes.text: My driving ankle.
jokes.setup: Marge trying to drive to help the family
jokes.punchline: Claiming to have injured her 'driving ankle'
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist excuse with perfect economy and delivery

jokes.text: Oh, no! The roast beef is making them stronger! And the falafel is making them angrier!
jokes.setup: Throwing food to distract attacking animals
jokes.punchline: Each food having specific negative effects on the predators
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with specific food effects that makes logical nonsense

jokes.text: Oh, wait. It's Blatz.
jokes.setup: Homer refusing to sacrifice his beer to save family
jokes.punchline: Changing his mind when he realizes it's cheap beer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer characterization - willing to sacrifice family for good beer but not bad beer

jokes.text: You know, we've been through some 280 adventures together, but our bond has never been stronger.
jokes.setup: Family bonding moment after surviving animal attack
jokes.punchline: Homer breaking fourth wall by referencing episode count
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor breaking fourth wall while maintaining character voice

jokes.text: Could this be the end of our series of events?
jokes.setup: Family seeming to have resolved their issues
jokes.punchline: Lisa also breaking fourth wall but with TV terminology
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to Homer's meta moment with Lisa's more sophisticated phrasing

jokes.text: Oh, it's the woman I married in Vegas!
jokes.setup: Someone at the door asking for Homer
jokes.punchline: Revelation of Vegas wife arriving to disrupt happy ending
jokes.explanation: Perfect plot twist that completely upends the resolution, excellent structure

jokes.text: Homer, you're a bad man, and your seed should be wiped from the earth. No offense, children.
jokes.setup: Vegas wife confronting Homer
jokes.punchline: Politely apologizing to kids after wishing genocide on their father
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with perfect politeness/violence contrast

jokes.text: If I had known there were loose women in Las Vegas, I would never have let you go!
jokes.setup: Homer's Vegas excuse
jokes.punchline: Marge sarcastically expressing shock about Vegas having loose women
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic comeback highlighting Homer's obviousness about Vegas.

jokes.text: Liquors drunkened me!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to explain Vegas marriage wasn't his fault
jokes.punchline: Grammatically mangled excuse blaming alcohol
jokes.explanation: Good Homer malapropism showing his attempt to sound formal while making excuses

jokes.text: Upstairs? I hit the jackpot!
jokes.setup: Being shown basic shower facilities
jokes.punchline: Treating indoor plumbing as luxury gambling win
jokes.explanation: Character establishing low standards through gambling metaphor.

jokes.text: We never ever made whoopee, or even mouth whoopee!
jokes.setup: Homer defending his innocence
jokes.punchline: Childish euphemisms for sexual acts
jokes.explanation: Homer's childish way of denying physical intimacy fits character.

jokes.text: Mr. Simpson, under Nevada law, bigamy, or 'Mormon Hold 'Em,' is perfectly legal.
jokes.setup: Homer seeking annulment in court
jokes.punchline: Judge's casual poker reference for polygamy
jokes.explanation: Clever gambling/marriage pun that's both topical and quotable

jokes.text: You can't kick me out. It'll cause a miscount in the census!
jokes.setup: Homer being evicted from his house
jokes.punchline: Claiming census concerns as reason to stay
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic - grasping at bureaucratic straws to avoid consequences

jokes.text: Oh, no. She's making him a sandwich!
jokes.setup: Amber making suggestive sounds while doing something to Homer
jokes.punchline: Marge's horrified realization it's innocent food preparation
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection playing with audience expectations and Marge's jealousy

jokes.text: We don't use the 'I' word in this house.
jokes.setup: Ginger asking to 'Irish up' her coffee
jokes.punchline: Treating 'Irish' as profanity
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor showing Ned's extreme sensitivity to anything potentially offensive

jokes.text: We flushed your sin sticks down to hell. Smokers are jokers.
jokes.setup: Ginger looking for cigarettes
jokes.punchline: Ned's religious terminology for cigarettes and rhyming judgment
jokes.explanation: Good Ned characterization but relies on simple rhyming for the humor

jokes.text: You could look seven. And I could teach you to count cards.
jokes.setup: Offering to teach Lisa makeup
jokes.punchline: Suggesting making child look younger and teaching gambling
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment showing Ginger's inappropriate influence

jokes.text: No. You're thinking of someone with two knives.
jokes.setup: Moe saying someone with two wives should be happy
jokes.punchline: Bart's dark wordplay correction
jokes.explanation: Clever dark wordplay with excellent economy, though character integration is off for Bart

jokes.text: Why don't you just take Britney Beers and get out of here?
jokes.setup: Marge angrily telling Homer to leave
jokes.punchline: Combining Britney Spears with beer reference for Amber
jokes.explanation: Decent pun but relies on celebrity name-drop without much additional wit

jokes.text: Talk about what? Sports? Bigamy? Not a sports fan, huh?
jokes.setup: Marge wanting to talk seriously
jokes.punchline: Homer deflecting through absurd topic suggestions
jokes.explanation: Homer's classic deflection technique with absurd false equivalency.

jokes.text: And hold the Beach!
jokes.setup: Ordering a Sex on the Beach cocktail
jokes.punchline: Requesting to hold the 'Beach' part of the drink name
jokes.explanation: Simple sexual innuendo based on drink name - functional but predictable

jokes.text: You know, she can put that mole anywhere on her face.
jokes.setup: Praising Amber's appearance
jokes.punchline: Treating removable beauty mark as positive feature
jokes.explanation: Decent visual gag observation but not particularly clever

jokes.text: You want to give Honest Abe another term in the Oval Office?
jokes.setup: Grampa talking to Amber about their wedding night
jokes.punchline: Presidential euphemism for sexual activity
jokes.explanation: Creative euphemism fitting Grampa's age and historical references

jokes.text: You crapped out, Vegas Mom!
jokes.setup: Amber ending up married to Grampa instead
jokes.punchline: Gambling terminology for her bad luck
jokes.explanation: Good use of gambling terminology with Vegas theme and efficient delivery

jokes.text: Yeah. I'm sure there's poker games we can be won in.
jokes.setup: Deciding to return to Vegas
jokes.punchline: Grammatically mangled reference to being wagered in poker
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate line showing Ginger's dim nature and gambling lifestyle

jokes.text: Well, it hurts now. But the senility will take care of that.
jokes.setup: Homer apologizing for Grampa losing another wife
jokes.punchline: Grampa looking forward to forgetting through mental decline
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor perfectly fitting Grampa's character and age-related issues

jokes.text: You know, I have a son about your age.
jokes.setup: Watching attractive woman leave
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate suggestion to set up his son
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Grampa's inappropriate but innocent nature
